title: 三只瞎老鼠
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\151-三只瞎老鼠-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Molly and Jules Davis discuss the mysterious death of Madame Lion and the police investigation regarding a person seen near the scene. Their conversation is interrupted by the arrival of Christopher Lane, a young man with light brown hair and light-colored eyes. Having traveled from Wales through severe winter weather, Lane speaks in an impatient, rapid-fire manner and expresses an intense interest in Victorian-style decor, such as four-poster beds and mahogany sideboards. He identifies himself as Christopher Reine, an aspiring architect.

While Jules expresses immediate dislike for Lane, calling him a "little bastard," Molly insists they remain professional because Lane is paying seven guineas a week to stay at the inn. After settling into the rose-red room, Lane assists Molly with cleaning in the kitchen.

The following morning, Mrs. Boyle arrives at the inn via taxi. An intimidating and domineering woman, Mrs. Boyle criticizes the establishment for not being a well-equipped or scientifically run business. Giles and Molly stand their ground against her aggressive attitude. Later that afternoon, Mrs. Boyle complains that Lane is too odd. The situation becomes more precarious when a local baker, dressed for extreme weather, warns the Davises that the roads are becoming blocked by snow. He notes that his return is unlikely for at least two days, suggesting the residents must rely on their existing reserves.
